It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Acronia stenota include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
